messages.content: Build a pure Decider for an insurance Policy aggregate in TypeScript. Export initialState, decide, and evolve. Use discriminated unions for Command, Event, and State, and throw a typed DomainError when activating an already-lapsed policy.
How do I model an insurance Claim aggregate as a Decider in TypeScript? Cover file, assess, approve, and reject commands with exhaustive evolve and typed errors.
Explain briefly how a Decider handles a Quote aggregate in event-sourcing, then implement one with prepare, present, accept, and expire transitions in strict TypeScript.

Write a Decider for a Contract aggregate in TypeScript. Constraints: strict types, no `any`, discriminated unions for Command/Event/State, exhaustive evolve with `never` default, and throw a typed error when terminating an already-terminated contract.
Build a Decider for a Vendor aggregate in TypeScript. Cover onboard, approve, suspend, and reactivate. Suspend from suspended must throw.
Explain briefly what a Decider does, then implement a Purchase aggregate Decider in TypeScript with place, fulfil, and cancel. Cancelling a fulfilled purchase must throw.
How do I build a projection that produces a PremiumOverview read model from Policy events in TypeScript? Show the project function and initial view.
Build a read-model projection for a ClaimStats view in TypeScript. It should track filedCount, approvedTotal, rejectedCount, and averageClaim. Include a typed project function and emptyView.

Write a projection that maintains a VendorDirectory view — id -> { name, status } — from Vendor events in TypeScript. Constraints: immutable record updates, exhaustive project with never default, ignore unknown fields.
Explain how a projection differs from evolve in event-sourcing, then implement a ContractLedger projection in TypeScript that tracks signed vs terminated counts plus total value.

Build a versioned Snapshot loader in TypeScript for a Policy aggregate. Include CURRENT_VERSION, two upgrader functions (v1->v2, v2->v3), and a loader that asserts the upgraded value matches CurrentState.
How do I implement snapshot loading with a version-upgrade chain for a Contract aggregate in TypeScript? Show two upgraders and a loader.
Write a Snapshot type and upgrade chain for a Quote aggregate in TypeScript. Constraints: no `any`, chained upgraders keyed by source version, throw a typed error on unknown version.

Build an optimistic-concurrency write path in TypeScript for a Policy stream. Declare a local EventStore interface with appendToStream and readStream, surface a typed ConcurrencyConflict when the server reports a mismatch, and use expectedRevision.
What's the TypeScript way to retry an appendToStream on optimistic concurrency conflict for a Vendor stream? Show a minimal example with expectedRevision and a typed conflict error.
Add optimistic-concurrency handling wrapped in Effect.gen to this Claim append path. Use an EventStore interface with expectedRevision, and surface ConcurrencyConflict on mismatch.

Build a CommandHandler for Policy commands in TypeScript. It must load current state via readStream + reduce(evolve, initial), call decide, and appendToStream with expectedRevision. Declare EventStore locally.
Explain the responsibilities of a CommandHandler over an EventStore, then implement one for a Claim aggregate in TypeScript.
Add Effect.gen-based orchestration to this Contract CommandHandler. Load the stream, run decide, and call appendToStream with expectedRevision.
Fix this Quote CommandHandler: business logic leaked into handle(), and appendToStream is missing expectedRevision. Refactor in TypeScript so handle only orchestrates.

Build a checkpointed subscription runner in TypeScript for a PremiumOverview projection. It must read the last checkpoint, apply each Policy event, and persist the new checkpoint AFTER the projection applies.
How do I make a Vendor projection subscription resume after a crash in TypeScript? Show CheckpointStore, a runSubscription loop, and explain why checkpoint write must follow projection apply.
Add named per-consumer checkpoints to this Contract subscription in TypeScript so multiple projections can share one event stream.
Build a generic aggregateStream helper in TypeScript that folds an async iterable of events into an aggregate state via an evolve function. Do not mutate the input; return the folded state.
How do I replay a Claim stream into state using an aggregateStream helper and a `when` reducer in TypeScript? Show a minimal example.
Write an aggregateStream helper and `when` reducer for an Agreement aggregate in TypeScript. Constraints: no mutation of inputs, exhaustive never default, uses reduce<> generic.
Add an Effect.gen-wrapped aggregateStream helper to this Purchase module. It should fold an async iterable into state using `when` with a never default.
Build an upcast function in TypeScript that migrates legacy Policy events (v1 shape) into the current Policy event union. Throw UnknownEventError on unrecognised shapes.

Write an upcast function for Claim events in TypeScript. Constraints: switch on `{ type, version }`, translate v1 ClaimFiled to current shape (new field `currency`), throw UnknownEventError as the default.

Build a resumable read-model rebuild in TypeScript for a Policy projection. It must read events in batches from a `from` revision, call project per event, persist the updated view, and advance a cursor.
Refactor this Claim rebuild function to support resume from a `from` revision and batch progress reporting. Keep the project call pure in TypeScript.

Build a CQRS boundary for Policy in TypeScript: a handleCommand that uses a Decider + EventStore and a queryView that reads a PolicyView from a read store. Note in a comment that the read store is eventually consistent.
How do I separate commands and queries for a Vendor aggregate in TypeScript? Show handleCommand using Decider + EventStore, and queryView reading from a VendorDirectory read store.

Build a process-manager skeleton in TypeScript that reacts to Quote events and issues Contract commands. Pure `react(event)` returning a Command or null, covering QuoteAccepted -> DraftContract and ContractSigned -> ArchiveQuote.

Explain briefly what a process-manager (saga) does in event-sourcing, then implement a stateful one for a Claim aggregate: ClaimFiled -> AssignAdjuster, ClaimAssessed -> ScheduleReview. Encode state as a pure (state, event) => { next; command? }.
Build a translate function in TypeScript that converts Policy domain events into integration events with a versioned schema field. Return null for internal-only events.

Write an appendWithOutbox function in TypeScript for Contract events. Constraints: local transactional interface, insert outbox rows AND appendToStream in one unit of work, comment explaining the publisher role.
What's the TypeScript way to define a typed event schema and a reduce<> helper that rebuilds aggregate state for an Agreement? Show a short example.
Build a projection for a Coverage read model in TypeScript that sums coverage amount across Policy events and tracks count of active coverages. Exhaustive project with never default.
Write an Underwriter Decider in TypeScript with assess, approve, and decline commands. Wrap a CommandHandler over an EventStore in Effect.gen. Exhaustive never default.
